title: Drop-Leaf Round Storage Table
summary: A rolling table with two open shelves and two fold-down leaves that swing up into a full round top — a side table that expands when you need it.
description:
This is a clever small-space table: a narrow storage cart with two open shelves that lives against a wall, then flips up two hinged leaves to become a round dining or game table. On casters, it rolls out and expands in seconds.

The base is straightforward 2x2-leg-and-shelf casework with pocket holes. The trick is the drop-in tip about pocket-hole direction: drill the top-apron pocket holes facing upward before assembly so you can grab the tabletop from underneath later. Hide the rest of the pocket holes to the insides.

The round top is made by building a rectangular center panel, hinging the two leaves to it so it becomes a big square when open, then drawing an arc across the whole thing and cutting the circle with a jigsaw — so the curve flows continuously across the seams. Two small warnings from the build: caster bolts can collide with the pocket screws in the bottom shelf trim, so plate-mount casters are safer than bolt-through ones, and buy proper drop-leaf support hardware (the 8" pairs) — they're not rock-solid but they install in seconds and are plenty sturdy for a table like this.

tools.note: Cuts the round top across the leaf seams in one arc.

materials.name: Basic hinges
materials.note: Hinge the two leaves to the center panel.

materials.name: 2" caster wheels
materials.note: Plate-mount, not bolt-through — the bolts can hit the shelf-trim pocket screws.

cutList.note: Two planks form the center panel, one plank each leaf.

steps.title: Build the two ends
steps.body: Build the two end assemblies, keeping insides flush and hiding the 3/4" pocket holes to the insides, joined with 1-1/4" pocket screws and glue. Drill 3/4" pocket holes facing upward on the insides of the top aprons now, for grabbing the tabletop later.

steps.title: Add the shelves
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes all around both shelves and attach them to the legs per the layout.

steps.title: Add the side trim and top aprons
steps.body: Add the side trim with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" screws, fastening the shelves to the corresponding trim as well. Remember to drill upward-facing pocket holes in the top aprons before you attach them, for the tabletop.

steps.title: Build the round top
steps.body: Build the center tabletop panel with a row of pocket holes down the center and glue. Hinge the two leaves to it so the whole thing opens into a big square. Draw an arc across the entire assembly, open, and cut the circle with a jigsaw so the curve flows across the leaf seams. Attach the center panel to the base through the upward pocket holes.

steps.title: Casters, leaf supports, and finish
steps.body: Add the four casters to the bottom — use plate-mount ones so their fasteners don't collide with the bottom-shelf trim pocket screws. Install the 8" drop-leaf supports under each leaf so it locks level when raised. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Rolling storage table with two drop leaves that form a round top
